rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location with direct beach access being the standout feature, consistently praised for being 'right on the beach' with stunning sunsets and peaceful coastal atmosphere Strong natural setting (18/20) with beautiful ocean views and off-leash beach section nearby, making it ideal for families and pet owners seeking a genuine beach holiday experience Significant cleanliness inconsistencies and security concerns including multiple theft incidents, variable customer service, and aging facilities that don't always match Big4 expectations or premium pricing
rationale_honourable: Sunset Beach Holiday Park earns its Silver rating primarily for its exceptional beachfront location and natural setting. Families consistently praise the direct beach access, stunning sunsets, and the peaceful atmosphere just steps from the ocean. While the park has good bones with clean facilities and friendly staff, aging amenities, inconsistent customer service experiences, and concerns about site maintenance and security issues prevent it from achieving a higher score.
water_fun: Small pool available, beach is main attraction
kids_play: Good playground with equipment for different ages
pet_detail: Pet friendly with dedicated dog friendly cabins available; off-leash beach section very close to park
best_for: This park suits families with young children who want easy beach access and outdoor play, are comfortable with basic facilities, and don't require extensive onsite entertainment or modern amenities
executive_summary:
PARK: Sunset Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 71/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 157

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (11/20) — Limited structured entertainment with reviews mentioning a playground with equipment for different age groups, BBQ areas, a small pool, and pool table in camp kitchen. No waterpark, jumping pillow, or organized kids activities mentioned. Families appreciate the playground upgrades but note the beach is the main entertainment drawcard.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural setting with direct beach access being the standout feature. Reviews consistently praise being "right on the beach," "steps from the ocean," stunning sunsets from the viewing platform, and the peaceful coastal environment. Some mention pink sand nearby and beautiful ocean views. Minor concerns about beach erosion and dead lawns in dry weather.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with many praising "spotlessly clean amenities" and thorough daily cleaning, but significant concerns raised about inconsistent standards. Multiple reviews mention filthy cabins with dog hair, spiders, cockroaches, mold, dust, and unidentified stains. Amenities described as "dated but clean" by most, though some report wet floors and maintenance issues.

Value (10/15) — Frequent complaints about pricing with reviews calling it "overpriced," "expensive for what it was," and "not worth it." Rates mentioned include $175-$249/night for cabins and $42-$60 for powered sites. Some guests feel facilities don't match the price point, though the beachfront location is seen as offsetting value concerns for many families.

Site Size (7/10) — Site sizes receive mixed feedback with some praising "spacious sites" and "good sized caravan sites," while others complain about cramped conditions, inability to use awnings on drive-through sites, and sites being "too narrow." Several reviews mention sites are "large enough" but just barely, with concrete slabs not always well-positioned.

Sentiment (8/10) — Generally positive sentiment with many repeat visitors and recommendations, though tempered by frustration about inconsistent service and facility quality. Common phrases include "we will be back," "highly recommend," and "loved our stay," but balanced by "never again" and disappointment from guests expecting higher Big4 standards.

Location (6/10) — Located about 10 minutes north of Geraldton CBD, which is both praised for being away from traffic noise and criticized for being less central. Walking distance to IGA supermarket (15-20 minutes) is appreciated. Direct beach access is the major location strength, though some note the beach itself is "nothing special" compared to town beaches.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Significant cleanliness inconsistencies with multiple reports of filthy cabins, pest problems (spiders, cockroaches, ants), and unacceptable accommodation conditions that resulted in guests leaving immediately and requesting refunds.

2. Security and theft concerns with multiple documented incidents of items stolen from vehicles including Engel fridges and bicycles, with management providing inadequate warnings or responses to safety issues.

3. Variable and often poor customer service experiences including rude reception staff, interrogation-style check-ins, inflexible policies, lack of warm welcomes, and staff who appear disinterested or unhelpful to paying guests.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"right on the beach," "spotlessly clean amenities," "great playground for the kids," "facilities are dated but clean," "overpriced," "one of the filthiest places I've ever seen," "our Engel freezer was stolen," "uninviting cranky receptionist," "dog friendly cabins," "beautiful sunsets," "staff were very friendly," "walking distance to IGA"

rationale_top3: Exceptional natural setting with spectacular sunset views over the Indian Ocean and peaceful coastal surroundings just a 2-10 minute walk from the beach Large, flat caravan sites with plenty of space for kids to ride bikes and play, combined with recent significant renovations improving cabin quality and cleanliness Good value for money with affordable rates, family-friendly pool, and convenient location as a stopover 10-15 minutes from Geraldton
rationale_honourable: Drummond Cove Holiday Park earns Silver recognition primarily for its stunning coastal location with spectacular sunset views over the Indian Ocean and the significant recent renovations that have transformed many cabins into modern, well-appointed accommodations. Recent family reviews highlight the pool, playground, and spacious grassed areas perfect for kids to run and play, combined with friendly staff and large, flat caravan sites that provide good value for families seeking a peaceful beachside stopover near Geraldton.
water_fun: Pool available, crystal clear and family-friendly
kids_play: Small playground, occasional activities like Easter movies
pet_detail: Pet friendly accommodation available with small dogs welcomed
best_for: This park best suits families with young children who value natural coastal beauty, spacious sites, and peaceful surroundings over structured entertainment and are comfortable with a 10-15 minute drive to town facilities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Drummond Cove Holiday Park
SCORE: 69/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 58

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (11/20) — Limited on-site entertainment with a small, dated playground and occasional activities like Easter movie nights and kids activities mentioned by one reviewer; most families noted the park is better for outdoor play with space for bike riding on grassed areas rather than structured entertainment programs.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural setting overlooking the Indian Ocean with spectacular sunset views, walking trail to the beach (2-10 minute walk depending on fitness), peaceful coastal surroundings, and beautiful open spaces; reviews consistently praised the "stunning views," "beautiful ocean views," and tranquil location surrounded by nature.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed results with recent reviewers praising "spotless clean" amenities and "significantly updated" modern cabins, while older reviews (2023-2024) reported serious cleanliness issues including cockroach infestations, dirty blankets, mouse droppings, and unmaintained facilities; renovations appear to be addressing historical problems.

Value (12/15) — Generally good value with reviewers noting "excellent value for money" and "affordable" rates, though some noted price increases from $40 to $65 for powered sites caused concern; one negative review felt $150-$170 per night was excessive given past cabin conditions, but recent renovations seem to have improved value perception.

Site Size (9/10) — Consistently praised for "sites are huge," "nice and large" caravan sites, "big enough" spaces, and "plenty of parking"; families appreciated flat, grassed sites with enough room for kids to ride bikes and play.

Sentiment (8/10) — Overall positive sentiment in recent reviews with many saying they'd return and recommending the park, particularly praising staff friendliness and the view; however, several harsh 1-star reviews about past conditions, pricing disputes, and management issues tempered the overall sentiment score.

Location (9/10) — Located 10-15 minutes outside Geraldton with easy highway access, close to beach (2-10 minute walk), peaceful out-of-town setting with minimal road noise despite highway proximity; "great stopover spot" that's "quiet" and "tranquil" but requires driving to town facilities.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Entertainment facilities are minimal for families—the playground is described as "small and old, needs updating" with "no playground facilities" mentioned in one detailed cabin review, and organized activities appear rare except for occasional holiday events.

2. Significant historical cleanliness and maintenance issues documented in 2023-2024 reviews including cockroach infestations, bed bugs, flooding bathrooms, power outages, and "dirty old cabins" that created serious concerns, though recent renovations appear to be addressing these problems gradually.

3. Inconsistent accommodation quality with cabin conditions varying dramatically depending on whether renovated or not, lack of wifi access, outdated facilities in unrenovated units, and operational issues like pool closures without advance notice disappointing families who specifically booked for those amenities.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spectacular sunset views," "playground and pool area so children have space to enjoy and play," "they put on a movie and activities for kids over Easter," "enough space for kids to ride their bikes," "sites are huge," "significantly updated," "spotless clean," "children's playground small and old needs updating," "cockroaches on the kitchen bench," "kids enjoyed pool a lot"

rationale_top3: Persistent and well-documented theft problem with car break-ins, smashed windows, stolen bikes and belongings occurring regularly despite warnings from staff; multiple reviews report police attendance and describe theft as notorious Severely outdated and poorly maintained older amenities blocks described as disgusting with flooding showers, sewage smells, dirty water flow between cubicles, broken locks, crumbling walls, and facilities dating back to the 1970s Intrusive 24-hour industrial noise from adjacent port operations including train horns, rail shunting, cargo loading, and truck traffic making sleep extremely difficult with families reporting being woken 2 to 5 times per night
rationale_honourable: Direct beach access across the road with walking paths to lighthouse and dog beach nearby, along with helpful and friendly staff who provide good service despite challenging park conditions
water_fun: Pool available but limited entertainment features
kids_play: Playground, jumping pillow, beach access nearby
pet_detail: Dog friendly with off-leash beach area across the road
best_for: Budget-conscious families with self-contained rigs who prioritise beach proximity over on-site entertainment and can manage older facilities
executive_summary:
PARK: Geraldton Belair Gardens Caravan Park
SCORE: 48/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-25
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 323

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews mention pool, jumping pillow, and playground facilities with bouncing cushion for kids, but limited structured activities or water features beyond basic pool; majority of reviews focused on beach access rather than on-site entertainment.

Nature (14/20) — Park is directly across from Pages Beach with walking paths to lighthouse, dog beach nearby, dolphins spotted, shady casuarina trees throughout sites; however, industrial port setting significantly detracts from natural ambience.

Cleanliness (6/15) — Deeply polarised reviews: newer amenities blocks described as "spotless" and "very clean" but older blocks repeatedly called "disgusting," "ancient," "cracked walls," "1960s prison amenity block," with sewage smells, flooding showers, and dirty water flowing between cubicles; potholes throughout roads.

Value (5/15) — Numerous reviews cite overpricing for condition: "$60 powered site fee...a rip off," "$200.00 for this is a disgrace," "most expensive ensuite site" with no concrete slab, "way too expensive for what it is," with theft costs adding to guest expenses.

Site Size (7/10) — Reviews mention "large spots," "good sized sites," "roomy" sites with concrete slabs, "double concrete site slabs," and "lots of space," though some note "tight" caravan sites and narrow spots in older sections.

Sentiment (4/10) — Severely damaged by security concerns with multiple reviews stating "never stay again," "won't be back," "would not recommend," "avoid this place," "notorious for burglaries"; theft incidents include car break-ins, smashed windows, stolen bikes, generators, and belongings; positive comments from repeat visitors outweighed by safety fears.

Location (4/10) — Beach access praised as "just across the road" with lighthouse and walking paths, but industrial port setting with 24-hour train shunting, cargo ships, rail yards, and truck noise heavily criticised; 5-minute drive to town but isolated from restaurants and shopping; "middle in industriegebied."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Persistent and well-documented theft problem with car break-ins, smashed windows, stolen bikes and belongings occurring regularly despite warnings from staff; multiple reviews report police attendance and describe theft as "notorious" and "very regular occurrence."

2. Severely outdated and poorly maintained older amenities blocks described as "disgusting," "worst toilets I have used," with flooding showers, sewage smells, dirty water flow between cubicles, broken locks, crumbling walls, and facilities dating back to the 1970s requiring complete rebuilding.

3. Intrusive 24-hour industrial noise from adjacent port operations including train horns, rail shunting, cargo loading, truck traffic, and reverse beepers making sleep extremely difficult, with numerous families reporting being woken "2 to 5 times a night" despite the park having no control over this.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"car window got smashed and stole valuable belongings," "thefts are common here and the reception won't tell you," "our electric bikes were stolen while locked together," "trains shunting all night," "disgusting toilets and showers," "worst toilet/shower block we've seen," "beach just across the road," "staff were amazing, super friendly"
